page: Amazon Web Services g4ad.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. This x86_64 architecture system features 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading running at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The server is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

For accelerated workloads, the instance includes a single AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided via a 600 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and network connectivity is rated at an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. The system runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 6134.32 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8094.0. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core performance falling in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth for large, potentially uncached blocks is also limited, scoring in the bottom 25% for both read and write operations, while small-block cached read bandwidth remains average.

Given the hardware profile, this server offers a cost-efficient option for graphics-intensive applications, remote workstations, and moderate parallel processing tasks that benefit from GPU acceleration without requiring high-end CPU single-core speeds. It is suited for workloads such as 3D rendering, video transcoding, and light-to-medium LLM inference, where the AMD Radeon Pro GPU and local NVMe storage can be leveraged.
description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features a dedicated AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM and a 600 GB local NVMe SSD. While single-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth are relatively weak, the instance delivers average multi-core CPU and cached memory performance. This hardware profile makes it a cost-efficient option for graphics-intensive workloads, remote desktop hosting, and moderate parallel processing tasks.

tagline: An AMD-powered virtual server featuring dedicated Radeon Pro graphics and local NVMe storage for graphics-intensive and parallel computing workloads.
